reference: Yes, I am a physician, but I am asking about my insulin-dependent husband, age sixty-four, Hispanic, who has had insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus for thirty-five years. He has good control but is experiencing slight pain in his left arm, similar to a spasm, which has subsided but has left him with what he describes as a slight strain. It does not sound like a myocardial infarction, but I still wonder if we need to get an electrocardiogram now. It is not sounding like a classic myocardial infarction, but with his insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus, I am worried.
